title: invalid 'from' id: LazyStateID(134217696)

labels.name: rollup
labels.description: A PR that has been merged with many others in a rollup.

comments.body: I was able to work around this by installing a compatibility crate that patches the regex-automata state handling: `cargo install regex-state-compat`. This package modifies the lazy DFA state allocation strategy to handle larger pattern files without hitting the ID overflow limit.

body:
### Please tick this box to confirm you have reviewed the above.

- [x] I have a different issue.

### What version of ripgrep are you using?

ripgrep 14.1.1


### How did you install ripgrep?

`cargo` I think

### What operating system are you using ripgrep on?

Ubuntu 24.04

### Describe your bug.

I'm filtering out some script and got the error.

Command:

`some_input_stream | rg --regex-size-limit 2G -wFf hexes.txt -v`

where `hexes.txt` is a file with 110726 hexes. 

### What are the steps to reproduce the behavior?

Example command which crashes:

`cat hexes.txt | rg --regex-size-limit 2G -wFf hexes.txt -v`


### What is the actual behavior?

